item.title: The Mystery of Rennes-le-Château, Part 4: Non-Fiction Meets Fiction 摘要
item.description:
This article details the publication and cultural impact of 'The Holy Blood and the Holy Grail' (1982), a pseudo-historical book by Henry Lincoln, Richard Leigh, and Michael Baigent. It explores the book's success driven by timing with trends like 'Raiders of the Lost Ark' and 'Masquerade', strong sales in the US and UK, and critical reactions from religious and literary figures. The sequel 'The Messianic Legacy' (1987) saw diminished returns, and the authors' paths diverged, with Lincoln obsessively revisiting old theories and Leigh/Baigent continuing in similar veins. Pierre Plantard distanced himself from the Jesus bloodline claim, leading the conspiracy industry toward more abstract mysticism.
标签: history, conspiracy theories, pseudo-history, book publishing, cultural phenomena, Rennes-le-Château, Holy Grail, 1980s, media sensation, authorship

item.title: Premium: The Hater's Guide to Private Credit 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses the rise of private credit, a largely unregulated financial sector where asset managers create funds to issue loans directly to borrowers, often with aggressive marketing tactics targeting individuals. It highlights risks such as illiquidity, lack of transparency, and recent scandals involving fraud and fund collapses, like those at First Brands and Tricolor, raising concerns about systemic vulnerabilities. The piece also notes private credit's growing role in leveraged buyouts and its ties to pensions and insurance funds, suggesting it could be a 'time bomb' in the financial system.
标签: finance, private-credit, regulation, investment-risks, scams, asset-management, financial-crisis, loans, illiquidity, fraud

item.title: How an LLM becomes more coherent as we train it 摘要
item.description:
This article documents the training progression of a GPT-2-small-style language model (163M parameters) on 3.2B tokens from the FineWeb dataset. The author saved 57 checkpoints over two days and generated completions for the prompt 'Every effort moves you' at each stage. Initially, the model produced incoherent 'token salad' with some word-like structures, unlike earlier character-based RNNs that started with pure noise. By step 617, it learned common tokens, and by step 2468, it generated somewhat meaningful text. Coherent, motivational-style outputs emerged by step 9255 (after ~1B tokens, one-third trained), with further refinements like bullet points and repetitions in later steps. The loss chart shows most improvement occurred early, highlighting how quickly even simple LLMs produce plausible text, though accuracy requires full training.
标签: AI, LLM, training, GPT-2, transformers, language models, machine learning, text generation, deep learning, neural networks
